title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

description: Imagine the fragrant aroma of freshly baked bread filling your kitchen, a golden crust giving way to a soft, airy interior. This Focaccia Genovese is a true Ligurian masterpiece, generously drizzled with olive oil and studded with aromatic rosemary. Perfect as an appetizer, side, or simply enjoyed on its own.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water, sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Mix and Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly oil a clean bowl with olive oil. Place the dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Prepare for Baking
instructions.text: Gently punch down the dough to release the air. Transfer it to a well-oiled 9x13 inch (23x33 cm) baking pan. Drizzle with a generous amount of olive oil and gently dimple the surface with your fingertips, creating small indentations.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Topping
instructions.text: Sprinkle the fresh rosemary leaves and flaky sea salt evenly over the dough. Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap and let it rise again for 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy. Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C) during the last 15 minutes of this rise.

instructions.title: Bake the Focaccia
instructions.text: Bake the focaccia in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown and cooked through. The crust should be crisp and the interior soft.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: If desired, drizzle with a little more olive oil immediately after removing from the oven for extra flavor and shine. Let the focaccia cool in the pan for a few minutes before transferring it to a wire rack. Slice into squares or rectangles and serve warm.

tips: For extra flavor, use a high-quality extra virgin olive oil from Liguria for both the dough and the final drizzle.
Do not skip the dimpling step; it creates those characteristic pockets that hold the olive oil and flaky salt, enhancing both texture and taste.
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh on the day it is baked. If you have leftovers, store them at room temperature in an airtight container for up to 2 days, reheating briefly in the oven for best results.
article:
There are few aromas as inviting as freshly baked bread, and when that aroma is infused with the herbaceous perfume of rosemary and the rich fruitiness of olive oil, you know you’re in for a treat. Welcome to the world of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil, a simple yet profoundly satisfying Italian flatbread that embodies the culinary heart of Liguria. More than just a side dish, this focaccia is a testament to the beauty of quality ingredients and time-honored baking traditions. It’s a delightful journey for your senses, promising a crispy, golden crust, an irresistibly airy and tender crumb, and pockets of savory goodness from generous dimpling. If you've ever dreamt of bringing a piece of true Italian baking into your kitchen, this classic is where you begin.

## What is Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil?

At its core, focaccia is a flat oven-baked Italian bread, similar in style and texture to pizza dough. However, the "Genovese" designation elevates it to a specific regional specialty, hailing from Genoa, the capital city of Liguria. What makes Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil distinct is its generous use of extra virgin olive oil, both in the dough and as a lavish topping, creating a uniquely moist interior and a beautifully crisp, golden exterior. The surface is characteristically dimpled, creating small wells that hold pools of that precious olive oil, along with coarse sea salt and fragrant fresh rosemary.

Unlike other focaccias that might be heavily topped with various vegetables or cheeses, the Genovese style is celebrated for its elegant simplicity. The focus is purely on the quality of the flour, the yeast, and most importantly, the olive oil and rosemary. It's traditionally eaten plain, often for breakfast or as a snack, sometimes dipped in coffee (yes, really!). Its light, airy texture and rich flavor make it incredibly versatile, a staple of Italian baking that has charmed palates worldwide.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil requires a handful of simple, high-quality ingredients. Each plays a crucial role in achieving the desired texture and flavor.

*   **Strong Bread Flour (or All-Purpose):** About 500 grams. Bread flour, with its higher protein content, yields a chewier, more structured crumb. All-purpose flour can work, but the texture might be slightly less airy.
*   **Warm Water:** Around 400-450 ml (about 1 ¾ – 2 cups). The exact amount can vary based on flour type and humidity, so start with less and add if needed. Water should be lukewarm (around 105-115°F / 40-46°C) to activate the yeast.
*   **Active Dry Yeast:** 7 grams (about 2 ¼ teaspoons). This is your leavening agent, giving the focaccia its signature airy structure.
*   **Granulated Sugar:** 1 teaspoon. A little sugar helps to "wake up" the yeast.
*   **Fine Sea Salt:** 10 grams (about 1 ½ teaspoons) for the dough. Essential for flavor balance.
*   **High-Quality Extra Virgin Olive Oil:** At least 100 ml (about ½ cup), plus more for oiling the pan and drizzling. This is non-negotiable for authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil. Its flavor is a core component.
*   **Fresh Rosemary:** 2-3 sprigs, needles removed and roughly chopped. For that unmistakable aromatic touch.
*   **Flaky Sea Salt:** For sprinkling on top, providing bursts of briny crunch.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a rewarding process that doesn't require extensive kneading. Patience and a gentle touch are key.

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm water and sugar. Sprinkle the active dry yeast over the top. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix the Dough:** Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Stir with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy, sticky dough forms. No need to knead extensively at this stage. It will be quite wet – embrace the stickiness!
3.  **First Rise (Bulk Fermentation):** Cover the bowl tightly with plastic wrap or a damp kitchen towel. Let the dough rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until it has at least doubled in size. For a more developed flavor, you can also cold-proof it in the refrigerator overnight (8-18 hours).
4.  **Prepare the Pan:** Liberally drizzle a good amount of extra virgin olive oil (about ¼ cup) into a 9x13 inch baking pan or a similar size. Ensure the entire bottom and sides are well coated.
5.  **Transfer and Dimple:** Gently pour the risen dough into the prepared pan. Using well-oiled fingertips, gently spread the dough to fill the pan, stretching it slowly. Don't force it. Once spread, use your fingertips to create deep dimples all over the surface of the dough, pushing through to the bottom of the pan.
6.  **Second Rise and Toppings:** Drizzle more extra virgin olive oil generously over the dimpled dough. Scatter the chopped fresh rosemary and a good sprinkle of flaky sea salt over the top. Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a clean kitchen towel and let it rise again for 30-45 minutes in a warm spot, or until puffy.
7.  **Preheat and Bake:** While the dough is on its second rise, preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Bake the Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil for 20-30 minutes, or until it's golden brown on top and cooked through. The bottom should also be nicely browned.
8.  **Finish and Serve:** As soon as it comes out of the oven, drizzle a final touch of extra virgin olive oil over the hot focaccia. This enhances the flavor and keeps the crust tender. Let it cool for a few minutes in the pan before transferring to a cutting board. Slice into squares or rectangles and serve warm.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil isn't just about following steps; it's about understanding the nuances.

*   **Embrace High Hydration:** The dough for focaccia is meant to be wet and sticky. Don't be tempted to add too much flour, as this will result in a dense, tough bread. A high hydration level is key to its characteristic open, airy crumb.
*   **Quality Olive Oil is Paramount:** This cannot be stressed enough. The flavor of your Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is heavily dependent on the quality of your extra virgin olive oil. Invest in a good bottle – you'll taste the difference.
*   **Long Fermentation (Cold Proofing):** While a 2-hour rise works, an overnight cold proof in the refrigerator (after the initial mix) develops a significantly deeper, more complex flavor and a superior texture. The cold slows down the yeast activity, allowing enzymes to break down starches into sugars, enhancing both taste and browning.
*   **Gentle Dimpling:** When dimpling, be firm enough to create deep wells that trap olive oil, but gentle enough not to deflate all the precious air bubbles. Your fingers should touch the bottom of the pan.
*   **Don't Skimp on Olive Oil:** From oiling the pan to drizzling before baking and again after, olive oil is a core ingredient. It contributes to the crispy crust, moist interior, and signature flavor.
*   **Hot Oven:** A hot oven helps the focaccia achieve a rapid rise (oven spring) and a beautiful golden crust.

## Serving Suggestions

Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in countless ways.

*   **Plain and Simple:** The most traditional way to enjoy it is warm, straight from the oven, perhaps with an extra drizzle of good olive oil.
*   **With Meals:** It makes an excellent accompaniment to soups, stews, roasted meats, or pasta dishes, perfect for soaking up delicious sauces.
*   **Appetizer or Snack:** Serve it alongside a platter of cured meats, cheeses, olives, and marinated vegetables for an authentic Italian antipasto.
*   **Sandwiches:** Slice it horizontally and fill it with your favorite sandwich fillings – prosciutto, mozzarella, tomatoes, or grilled vegetables.
*   **Dipping:** It's fantastic for dipping into balsamic vinegar, pesto, or even a simple olive oil and herb blend.
*   **Breakfast:** As mentioned, it's not uncommon in Liguria to enjoy a piece of focaccia with coffee for breakfast!

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations to suit your taste.

*   **Different Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional, you can substitute or add other herbs like fresh thyme, oregano, sage, or a mix of Italian herbs.
*   **Added Toppings:** Before the second rise, you can press in cherry tomatoes (halved), sliced olives (Kalamata or Castelvetrano work well), thinly sliced onions (caramelized first for extra sweetness), or minced garlic.
*   **Cheese:** Sprinkle some grated Parmesan, Pecorino Romano, or even small chunks of fresh mozzarella over the dough before baking for a cheesy twist.
*   **Spice:** A pinch of red pepper flakes can add a subtle kick.
*   **Gluten-Free:** While possible with gluten-free flour blends, be aware that the texture will be significantly different from traditional focaccia due to the lack of gluten development.
*   **Sweet Focaccia:** Less common for the Genovese style, but some focaccias can be made sweet with toppings like grapes, figs, or a sprinkle of sugar.

Mastering the art of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a journey into the heart of Italian baking. With its humble ingredients and straightforward method, it delivers an extraordinary experience – a symphony of textures and flavors that will transport you straight to the sun-drenched Ligurian coast. Whether you're a seasoned baker or a curious beginner, this recipe promises delicious rewards and the immense satisfaction of creating something truly special with your own hands. So, roll up your sleeves, embrace the olive oil, and prepare to bake a masterpiece.
